summary.summary_3_sentences: This Exhibit 99.1 is a memorandum dated January 22, 2021 from Dechert LLP to the Apollo Conflicts Committee regarding an investigation into the relationship between Leon Black and Jeffrey Epstein, and any relationship between Epstein and Apollo Global Management, Inc. The Committee retained Dechert in October 2020 without restrictions, and the memorandum summarizes the firm's findings. Over three months, Dechert reviewed over 60,000 documents from Black, Apollo, Elysium Management LLC (Black’s family office), and Paul, Weiss, including emails, text messages, banking statements, and other communications.
main_organizations: Paul, Weiss, Rifkind, Wharton & Garrison LLP
key_findings: In October 2020, the Apollo Conflicts Committee retained Dechert LLP as independent counsel to investigate the relationship between Leon Black and Jeffrey Epstein, any work performed by Epstein for Black or his affiliates, any relationship between Apollo or its affiliates and Epstein or his affiliates, and any statements by or on behalf of Black or Apollo characterizing those relationships.
The Committee placed no restrictions on Dechert’s investigation.

Over three months, Dechert reviewed more than 60,000 documents, including emails, text messages, banking statements, and other communications, sourced from Black, Apollo, Elysium Management LLC, and Paul, Weiss.

Potentially relevant documents were collected from Apollo for current and former employees dating back to 1998, as well as for all current and former employees of the Family Office, including periods prior to the separate Elysium email address.
The memorandum states it summarizes Dechert’s findings.
